source.description: One real Claude Code session against a disposable synthetic stdio MCP server wrapped by the production Pinpoint CLI gateway.
source.persistedData: Only this content-free receipt is retained. The Claude stream, debug log, provider credentials, and temporary MCP state are not persisted.

method.task: Find one email by accountId through an MCP server and return only the value.
method.upstreamConstraint: The synthetic upstream tool intentionally exposes no filtering parameter and returns 1,000 records under structuredContent.data.accounts.
method.grader: The run passes only if the real client calls both MCP tools, no model-visible tool result exceeds 5,000 characters, and the final result exactly equals the expected synthetic email.

limitations: This is one first-party synthetic task, not a prevalence estimate or customer-production trace.
The prompt explicitly asked Claude to use the accounts MCP server; unrelated filesystem and shell tools were denied to prevent an alternate answer path.
The upstream server intentionally lacked a filter parameter, representing a poorly bounded tool rather than every MCP server.
The result proves client compatibility and autonomous follow-up querying for this task. It does not establish quality across free-form summarization, media, errors, or ambiguous data.
Character reduction is not provider token or bill reduction. Observed provider cost includes discovery and both MCP turns.
